seoTitle: Edit an Invoice Template in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Update your QuickBooks Online invoice template's branding, columns, and payment options so future bills match your business's current look.
intro: Business needs change, and your invoice template should keep up. This guide covers how to open an existing QuickBooks Online invoice style and edit its branding, content layout, and payment settings so every future bill stays accurate and on-brand.

steps.heading: Access Custom Form Styles
steps.body: Click the gear icon and choose Custom Form Styles to see your list of existing invoice templates.

steps.heading: Open the style you want to change
steps.body: Select the invoice template you already use to open it in the design editor.

steps.heading: Update visuals and branding
steps.body: Adjust your logo placement, swap color swatches, or change fonts in the Design tab to keep the look current.

steps.heading: Edit content fields
steps.body: In the Content tab, rewrite column labels, uncheck unused rows, or restructure line-item tables to match your process.

steps.heading: Preview and save changes
steps.body: Check the live preview to confirm calculations and layout align, then click Done to save your updated template for future invoices.

faq.question: Will editing my invoice template affect invoices already sent?
faq.answer: No, previously sent invoices keep their original layout; edits only apply to invoices created after the change.

faq.question: Can I turn on payment links while editing the template?
faq.answer: Yes, the Emails tab lets you enable direct payment link buttons for bank transfer or credit card payments.

faq.question: Can I set the edited template as my default?
faq.answer: Yes, you can mark any saved custom style as the default so it's automatically applied to new invoices.

faq.question: What if my changes break the layout alignment?
faq.answer: Use the live preview panel while editing to catch column or spacing issues before saving.
